title: Tender Toasted Russian Kinako Skillet
description: A fresh and crunchy solo dinner dish featuring kinako, cooked in a ttukbaegi earthenware skillet with Mediterranean-friendly ingredients. This recipe combines the flavors of Russia and Japan with a hint of Mediterranean spice.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Crack the eggs into a bowl and whisk them together with a fork.
Add the kinako to the eggs and mix until well combined.
Heat the olive oil in the ttukbaegi earthenware skillet over medium heat.
Pour the egg mixture into the skillet and cook until the edges start to set.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 10 minutes.
Remove the skillet from the oven and top with feta cheese, cherry tomatoes, kalamata olives, and artichoke hearts.
Return the skillet to the oven and bake for an additional 5 minutes.
Remove the skillet from the oven and sprinkle with chopped parsley and a squeeze of lemon juice.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
